paths.title: Solid Foundations
paths.description: Build intuition from regular solids, duality, and four-dimensional analogues.

paths.title: Coxeter Geometry
paths.description: Move from the 600-cell to projections, reflections, and E8 roots.

paths.title: Exceptional Bridges
paths.description: Explore the qualified relationships connecting E8 to McKay and H4 imagery.

paths.title: Rendering Mathematics
paths.description: Separate mathematical data from the visual techniques used to reveal it.

lessons.title: Why only five?
lessons.claimNote: The five-solid classification and dual pairs are standard results for convex regular polyhedra.

sources.title: Platonic Solid
sources.author: Wolfram MathWorld
sources.scope: definition, enumeration, and historical attribution of the five convex regular polyhedra

lessons.title: Into four dimensions
lessons.claimNote: Polytope counts and Schläfli notation are mathematical facts; the animated projection is an explanatory display choice.

sources.title: 600-Cell
sources.author: Wolfram MathWorld
sources.scope: 600-cell element counts and duality with the 120-cell

lessons.title: The 600-cell
lessons.claimNote: Element counts, duality, and the quaternion model are established; screen depth and motion are projection choices.

sources.title: 600-Cell
sources.author: Wolfram MathWorld
sources.scope: 600-cell element counts and duality with the 120-cell

lessons.title: The Coxeter plane
lessons.claimNote: The Coxeter projection and root orbits are canonical up to orientation and scale; glow and color are display choices.

sources.title: The E8 root system
sources.author: David Vogan, MIT Mathematics
sources.scope: 240 roots; eight projected circles with 30 roots at each radius

sources.title: Coxeter Planes
sources.author: John R. Stembridge, University of Michigan
sources.scope: definition of a Coxeter plane and order-h rotational action

lessons.title: Roots and reflections
lessons.claimNote: Root counts, inner products, and Weyl reflections come from the canonical E8 data; the interface highlighting is explanatory.

sources.title: The Character Table for E8 — Technical Details
sources.author: American Institute of Mathematics
sources.scope: E8 rank, dimension, root count, and Weyl-group order

lessons.title: McKay correspondence
lessons.claimNote: The McKay correspondence is established; transitions among solids, the 600-cell, and E8 are a qualified visual analogy, not a literal construction.

sources.title: McKay correspondence lecture notes
sources.author: University of Michigan
sources.scope: binary polyhedral groups, irreducible representations, and affine ADE McKay graphs

sources.title: Experimental evidence for the occurrence of E8 in nature and the radii of the Gosset circles
sources.author: Bertram Kostant
sources.scope: eight Gosset circles; Coxeter-element orbits; McKay dimension labels

lessons.title: The designed Bloom morph
lessons.claimNote: Bloom is an artistic interpolation created for this app. It is not a canonical deformation or mathematical map into E8.

sources.title: The E8 root system
sources.author: David Vogan, MIT Mathematics
sources.scope: 240 roots; eight projected circles with 30 roots at each radius

sources.title: Sphere tracing: A geometric method for the antialiased ray tracing of implicit surfaces
sources.author: John C. Hart
sources.scope: sphere tracing, signed distance bounds, implicit-surface rendering, and distance operations

lessons.title: Signed-distance rendering
lessons.claimNote: Sphere tracing and distance fields are established rendering techniques; this scene’s shapes and composition are app-designed.

sources.title: Sphere tracing: A geometric method for the antialiased ray tracing of implicit surfaces
sources.author: John C. Hart
sources.scope: sphere tracing, signed distance bounds, implicit-surface rendering, and distance operations
